explanation: The natural language requirement demands a self-test procedure ensuring full system and accessory functionality and correct operation of alarms. The clearest mapping in the given atomic propositions is to require that, globally (i.e., at every point), if the system is in self-test mode (SelfTestMode), then the self-test must pass ('selfTestPass' is true), and the local alarm test must also pass ('testAlarmsPass' is true). By using a conjunction, we can model that both self-test pass and alarm-test pass are required to deem the self-test procedure successful, thus capturing the intended meaning. Template 17 (G (bool_exp2 -> (G bool_exp3))) encodes that globally, whenever precondition bool_exp2 holds (SelfTestMode), then globally following, bool_exp3 must remain true (selfTestPass and testAlarmsPass). N_DURATION is not needed as the property requires global/unbounded checks.

The requirement states that the system must perform a self-test procedure which ensures both the system and its accessories are fully functional AND the alarms work. Given the available atomic propositions, 'selfTestPass' captures the success of all self-test steps ('indicates all self-test steps passed successfully'), and 'testAlarmsPass' indicates the alarms' test has passed. Therefore, an appropriate translation is to require that whenever the SelfTest procedure passes, also the alarms pass the test. This can be captured using the template G (bool_exp1 -> bool_exp3), where bool_exp1 = selfTestPass (all self-test steps passed) and bool_exp3 = testAlarmsPass (the local alarm test passes). N_DURATION is not required for this template.

The requirement specifies that the system's self-test procedure ensures both full system/accessory functionality and the proper operation of alarms. This matches with the atomic propositions 'selfTestPass' (all self-test steps successful) and 'testAlarmsPass' (alarms test passed). We want to state: Whenever SelfTestMode is active, it should eventually (within a bounded number of steps) be followed by both selfTestPass and testAlarmsPass becoming true. Template 14 is chosen: 'G (bool_exp1 -> (F[0,N_DURATION] bool_exp3))', where bool_exp1 is SelfTestMode, and bool_exp3 is the conjunction 'selfTestPass & testAlarmsPass'. N_DURATION is set to 10 as a concrete, finite bound. This template makes sense because it directly links activation of SelfTestMode to the eventual correct self- and alarm test results. bool_exp2 and bool_exp4 are unused in this template and therefore left empty.

The requirement is for a self-test procedure to ensure all system and accessory functions, including alarms, are working before operation. The most direct indicator is the atomic proposition 'selfTestPass', which signals successful completion of all self-tests. To ensure completeness and ongoing correctness, the best fitting LTL template is 'G selfTestPass', expressing that it's always the case that the self-test passes—meaning the system should not proceed unless the self-test of the system and its accessories (which, by implication, includes alarm function checks) passes successfully. If the self-test passes, system and alarms can be assumed verified functional. This meets the intent of ensuring function at start, with the template expressing that a requirement of selfTestPass is invariant before operation.

The requirement demands that the self-test procedure ensures the system and its accessories are fully functional, and that alarms work. The atomic propositions available are 'selfTestPass', which covers the successful completion of all self-test steps (system + accessories), and 'testAlarmsPass', which covers the proper functioning of alarms. The property should assert, whenever the system is in self-test mode (SelfTestMode), that within a bounded time, both 'selfTestPass' and 'testAlarmsPass' become true. This is best captured using template 14, which does not require bool_exp2. Here:
- bool_exp1: 'SelfTestMode' (SelfTest active)
- bool_exp3: '(selfTestPass & testAlarmsPass)' (System and alarm tests pass)

This globally requires that after entering SelfTestMode, both system and alarm self-tests pass within N_DURATION steps, fully matching the requirement.

The requirement states that the system must perform a self-test that ensures full functionality of both the system and its accessories, and that alarms work. According to the atomic propositions, 'SelfTestMode' indicates the self-test phase, while 'selfTestPass' means all steps in this procedure succeed. By using the LTL template 'G (SelfTestMode -> selfTestPass)' (template 30), we specify that whenever the system is in SelfTestMode, it is required that the self-test passes. This guarantees system and accessory functionality and implicitly the alarms, as the self-test procedure covers them. The relation is always enforced during self-test (not just at one time point), which matches the intent of the natural language statement.

The requirement mandates that a self-test procedure ensures the full functionality of the system and its accessories, as well as the proper functionality of the alarms. Thus, whenever SelfTestMode is active, the system must eventually satisfy both passing all self-test steps (selfTestPass) and passing alarm tests (testAlarmsPass) within a bounded duration. The best-fitting template is: G (bool_exp1 -> (F[0,N_DURATION] bool_exp3)), with bool_exp1 set to 'SelfTestMode' and bool_exp3 to '(selfTestPass & testAlarmsPass)', which expresses that whenever the self-test mode is active, a successful self-test (including alarms) must eventually occur soon after.

The natural language requirement states that the system must have a self-test procedure ensuring both the system and its accessories are fully functional and that alarms work. The atomic proposition 'selfTestPass' describes when all self-test steps are passed successfully, and 'testAlarmsPass' indicates local alarms test pass (success). To reflect the requirement, both of these conditions need to hold globally (i.e., always), ensuring that both the system and alarms are confirmed as functional, matching the self-test requirement. Thus, we use a conjunction inside a globally operator. The selected template is 'G (bool_exp3)', with bool_exp3 as '(selfTestPass & testAlarmsPass)'. N_DURATION is not needed for this template.

The requirement describes that a self-test should verify the functionality of the system, its accessories, and alarms. The atomic proposition 'selfTestPass' specifically indicates that all self-test steps passed successfully, corresponding directly to the natural language requirement. To relate this property to continuous system operation, we select a template that ensures that globally (always), the system only attests the self-test as passed when the self-test actually succeeded. Therefore, we express that globally, it should always be the case that selfTestPass is true. This covers the mandate that the system has a procedure (self-test), and only when the procedure passes, the system can be considered fully functional with alarms working. This also allows flexibility for re-testing on startup and before use, which are part of maintaining system integrity.

The requirement is that a self-test procedure shall ensure the system (and accessories) are fully functional, and that alarms work. The atomic proposition 'selfTestPass' captures successful completion of all self-test steps (system + accessories), and 'testAlarmsPass' captures that alarms have passed required tests. To formally specify that in SelfTestMode, these must eventually be true within a bounded period, use template 44 (G ((bool_exp1 & bool_exp2) -> (F[0,N_DURATION] bool_exp3))). Here, bool_exp1 is SelfTestMode, bool_exp2 is always true (tautology, so 'True'), and bool_exp3 is (selfTestPass & testAlarmsPass). N_DURATION=5 as a reasonable default. This expresses: whenever SelfTestMode is active, it will eventually (within duration) result in both a passed self-test and passed alarms.
